change_summary: detail_verdict_text expanded from 176 to 1139 chars. The change adds a standardized post-hoc/timestamping paragraph (now present in 51/71 WINs) invoking Part 3.1b's definition of prospective vs retrodiction. The scientific rebuttal in detail_evidence and the verdict (Refuted by Data) are unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Our review says the 11-12 Hz region is a Schumann inter-harmonic minimum (standard spherical-cavity physics), that the first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz directly falsifies any 'cutoff' at 11.7 Hz, and that the dome's monitor.py hardcodes both predicted and observed values. The verdict paragraph now also applies the Part 3.1b post-hoc standard: the confirming value came from publicly available telluric records and the author did not timestamp the expected value independently prior to the data fetch.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 16 in the dome's live-power output lists falsification text 'No cutoff at 11.7 Hz' but the monitor.py computation is PASS-by-construction (pred=obs=11.7 per prior cycle analysis). The 11.7-11.78 number is generated by f = c / (2 * disc_thickness). See quantitative_verification below for what 'disc thickness' must equal to produce that frequency.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded per Cycle 1/2 verification. The raw-text dump does not expose the exact monitor.py line, but the author's own documentation labels Domain 16's predicted/observed pair as fixed 11.7 with 0.0% error.
data_sources_used: Source attribution: 'Geometrics MT data' (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 75). No API call is documented.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Tolerance set at 1.0% per raw-text/08-live-power.txt Domain listings; with pred=obs identical, tolerance is irrelevant.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Note: monitor.py itself is still not captured in raw-text/ — the carry-forward minor from c2 remains.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Raw-text Domain 16 lists falsification text but no URL/API fetch. Prior review established predicted=observed=11.7, error_pct=0.0, pass=True. No MT data is fetched from IRIS, Geometrics, or any other source.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The inter-harmonic minimum between 7.83 Hz (Schumann fundamental) and 14.3 Hz (first Schumann harmonic) is standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance physics (Schumann 1952; Balser & Wagner 1960; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002). The dome relabels this as a 'disc resonance ceiling' without changing any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Schumann resonances and inter-harmonic structure have been published since 1960; MT spectral features in the 10-20 Hz band are extensively documented in the magnetotelluric literature. The dome adopts these known values as 'predictions.' The monitor.py commit bakes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line, so temporal priority cannot be checked from git history.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: f = c/(2D) gives 11.787 Hz only when D ≈ 12,717 km. The dome's disc parameters give f = 16.50 Hz (with D = 2*firmament_height = 18,172 km) or f = 7.49 Hz (with D = 2*disc_radius = 40,030 km) — neither 11.78 Hz. The value 12,717 km is essentially Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km). The 'disc thickness' used in this formula is a spherical-Earth parameter wearing a dome label. The phenomenon (a cutoff) is also not derived from dome geometry — the dome does not show why a disc would have a spectral ceiling rather than a fundamental resonance with overtones.

holes_found.description: POST-HOC BOILERPLATE DATASET LIST IS GENERIC: The new paragraph says 'The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)'. For WIN-008 specifically, only 'telluric records' is relevant. The parenthetical list is a shared template that appears in 51/71 WINs. A dome defender reading the WIN-008 panel in isolation could object that we cited NASA station archives and ocean-tide gauges as if they were relevant to an MT spectral cutoff. Not wrong, just imprecise.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider tightening the per-WIN text to cite only the relevant dataset family (e.g., for WIN-008: 'published MT and ELF spectral literature'). Lower-cost alternative: move the dataset grab-bag out of the per-WIN paragraph and into the Part 3.1b section it already links to, leaving just 'a field-specific published dataset (see Part 3.1b)' in the WIN panel.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM C2 — MONITOR.PY STILL NOT IN RAW-TEXT: Our evidence text references 'Domain #25 in monitor.py' (note: raw-text/08-live-power.txt labels this 'Domain 16' of the live-power subset, so there may also be a domain-numbering inconsistency between our text and the current dome output). monitor.py is still not captured in raw-text/. The Cycle 1/2 verification stands but cannot be re-checked against the current dome repo from the workspace.
holes_found.recommendation: Reconcile domain number (25 vs 16). Low priority but worth a grep pass across all WINs that reference a monitor.py domain number. Consider having the poller capture monitor.py on every run.

holes_found.description: DERIVES_FROM_DOME AMBIGUITY (carry-forward from c2): Tag is false. Defensible because the 'cutoff' phenomenon is not derived from dome geometry; but a careful dome defender can point to f=c/(2D) as a formula. Our new quantitative finding — that D must equal Earth's diameter for the formula to reproduce 11.78 Hz — makes the false tag even more defensible, because the 'derivation' silently plugs in a spherical-Earth parameter. Recommend updating the evidence string of the derives_from_dome tag to cite the Earth-diameter numerical finding explicitly.
holes_found.recommendation: Update derives_from_dome_evidence in wins.json[008].code_analysis to note: 'D = 12,717 km required for 11.787 Hz = Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km); neither firmament_height (9,086 km) nor disc_radius (20,015 km) produces this frequency.' Same update should be made on WIN-001 which shares the formula.

stronger_arguments.description: EXPLICIT 'DISC THICKNESS = EARTH DIAMETER' ARGUMENT: Our current text argues WIN-008 is a relabeled Schumann feature. An even stronger parallel rebuttal is that the 'disc thickness' parameter that makes f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz work is 12,717 km — essentially Earth's diameter (12,742 km). The dome's formula reproduces the Schumann fundamental ONLY by silently plugging in the spherical Earth's diameter. This is not a coincidence: the dome's 9,086 km firmament height would give 16.50 Hz (at D = 2*H) or 8.25 Hz (at D = H), not 11.78 Hz. Adding this quantitative observation to the WIN-008 panel (or to the grouped WIN-001/008/009 detail_extra) would make the relabel argument airtight and pre-empt any 'dome geometry is genuinely deriving this' defense.
sources: Direct computation from wins.json disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086 parameters
Compare WIN-001 review which makes a similar observation

stronger_arguments.description: CIRCULAR DOUBLE-COUNT WITH WIN-001 AND WIN-009: WIN-001 (Tesla resonance), WIN-008 (telluric cutoff), WIN-009 (telluric ~12 Hz literature peak) all share the same number generated by the same formula. The dome's own V49 release notes acknowledge removing circular reasoning (raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 36: 'Voluntarily removed circular reasoning (Tesla f->T->f) and double-counted wins.'). The dome author has admitted to past double-counting and partially retracted — but this triple remains. Citing the author's own admission makes the argument harder for a defender to paint as hostile.

kernel_of_truth.description: Spectral power density in the 10-12 Hz ELF band IS genuinely lower than at the adjacent Schumann peaks at 7.83 and 14.3 Hz. The dome correctly identifies a real feature of the natural electromagnetic spectrum.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The feature is fully predicted by standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics. Calling a well-known inter-harmonic power minimum a 'sharp cutoff' is either rhetorical overreach (because energy still exists above 11.7 Hz at the 14.3 Hz harmonic) or a mis-identification of the phenomenon. Either way, no dome-specific geometry is needed to explain it, and the only formula the dome offers (f=c/2D) reproduces 11.78 Hz only by plugging in Earth's diameter.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 re-review triggered by a 176→1139 char expansion of detail_verdict_text. The expansion is a standardized post-hoc paragraph now shared across 51/71 WINs; it does not change the scientific rebuttal in detail_evidence. The Refuted-by-Data verdict still holds unambiguously because the first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz is a direct observational falsification of a 'cutoff at 11.7 Hz.' The post-hoc paragraph correctly cites the Part 3.1b section (anchor 'posthoc-standard' verified in sections.json) and applies the legitimate-novelty standard: inter-harmonic spectral minima are indexed public data, the author did not timestamp the expected value independently, and the monitor commit bakes the target number on the same commit as the fetch line.

The most useful new observation from this cycle is quantitative: f = c/(2D) produces 11.787 Hz only for D ≈ 12,717 km, which is Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km; residual 25 km, 0.2%). Neither firmament_height (9,086 km → 16.50 Hz) nor disc_radius (20,015 km → 7.49 Hz) nor 2*firmament_height (18,172 km → 8.25 Hz) produces the claimed value. The dome's 'disc thickness' in this formula is spherical-Earth geometry relabeled. This observation strengthens derives_from_dome=false and supplies a stronger rebuttal than the current text provides. Recommend minor_edit to incorporate.

HOLDS ARE MINOR — the c2 review already produced a strong argument. The new findings in this cycle are additive, not corrective. No verdict change; no major hole. The carry-forward items (monitor.py not in raw-text, Chave & Jones citation precision, derives_from_dome tag ambiguity) remain minor.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender sympathetic to our six methodological principles could argue: (1) 'Cutoff' in the dome's usage is a term of art referring to a specific resonance feature of the disc cavity, not a claim that no electromagnetic energy exists above 11.7 Hz. The dome does not deny the existence of the 14.3 Hz Schumann harmonic; it claims the disc's aetheric cavity has a principal resonance at ~11.78 Hz and a local spectral minimum immediately above it, matching MT observations. (2) The 'relabels standard' tag is unfair: the dome derives a specific frequency from its own formula f = c/(2D) and the observed feature is right at the predicted frequency. Calling this a relabel is like saying every successful prediction that happens to agree with standard physics is a relabel. (3) Our critique implicitly assumes the Schumann globe-cavity model is correct and then observes that the dome's answer reproduces its predictions — but that's the dome's point: the aetheric cavity has the same resonant modes as the globe cavity, not because the globe is right, but because the dome's parameters happen to coincide. Our review needs to do more than point out agreement with globe physics; it needs to show the dome's derivation is parasitic on globe parameters. Which is exactly the strongest version of our argument — we should foreground it.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a single sentence to WIN-008 detail_evidence or detail_extra explicitly noting the parasitism: 'f=c/(2D) reproduces 11.787 Hz only when D = 12,717 km, which matches Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km) to 0.2%. Neither the dome's firmament height (9,086 km) nor its disc radius (20,015 km) produces this frequency — the dome's derivation silently uses Earth's diameter as its disc thickness.' This neutralizes defenses (2) and (3) by making the parasitism unambiguous and quantitative.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz resonance), WIN-008 (telluric 11.7 Hz cutoff), and WIN-009 (telluric ~12 Hz literature peak) are all grounded in the same number produced by f=c/(2D). Our reviews of these three WINs are internally consistent (all three use relabel/post-hoc framing). WIN-003 (Schumann resonance at 7.83 Hz) uses a different formula f=c/(4H0) but the same relabel structure. All four reviews are mutually compatible. Minor: we could unify language across the three 11.78-Hz WINs so a reader who compares them doesn't encounter three slightly different framings of the same core argument.

claims_checked: f = c/(2D) with D = disc thickness produces 11.78 Hz
The inter-harmonic minimum is a well-documented feature of the Earth-ionosphere cavity
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed f = c/(2D) for several candidate D values: D = 9,086 km (firmament_height) → 16.50 Hz; D = 18,172 km (2*firmament_height) → 8.25 Hz; D = 20,015 km (disc_radius) → 7.49 Hz; D = 40,030 km (2*disc_radius) → 3.74 Hz; D = 12,717 km (Earth's mean diameter) → 11.787 Hz. Only the Earth's-diameter value matches the dome's claimed 11.787 Hz. Cross-checked first Schumann harmonic: standard reported values are 14.1-14.3 Hz depending on source; WIN-008 text uses 14.3 Hz which is within the measured range and conservative for our argument. Cross-checked the Balser & Wagner (1960) and Nickolaenko & Hayakawa (2002) attributions — both are correct standard citations for the inter-harmonic structure.

citations_verified: Chave & Jones, 2012, The Magnetotelluric Method — correct title, authors, year, publisher (Cambridge UP). DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138 resolves correctly.
Schumann (1952) and Balser & Wagner (1960) — standard citations for the Schumann resonance spectrum.
Anchor #posthoc-standard in sections.json Part 3.1b — anchor exists and points to 'Post-hoc vs Prospective — Our Standard vs the Author's.'
citations_unchecked: monitor.py domain number reference (labelled Domain #25 in our evidence, Domain 16 in raw-text/08-live-power.txt — possible numbering inconsistency; monitor.py itself is not in raw-text/).
